pattern: Since every word has equal length, an edit is just a mismatched position, so a query qualifies when some dictionary word differs from it in at most two spots.
hints: All words are the same length, so reaching a dictionary word only ever means substituting letters, never inserting or deleting.
The number of edits between two equal-length words is the count of positions where they differ.
Keep a query if any dictionary word has at most two differing positions; preserve the original query order in the result.

edge_inputs.description: no query is within two edits
single-character words, any letter is one edit

stress.description: random queries against a dictionary, planting some near-matches
stress.note: half the queries are perturbations of dictionary words
